title: fix(memory): eliminate redundant createIndex call on semantic recall read path

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(memory): semantic recall search with non-default embedding dimensions

relatedIssues.title: [BUG] 15s latency in semantic recall despite having just one or two chat messages

summary: Fixes a performance regression where every semantic recall request triggers a redundant `createIndex` HTTP round-trip to the vector store. Two changes: (1) promotes the per-instance `indexValidationCache` to a module-level `globalIndexValidationCache` so serverless environments that create a new SemanticRecall instance per request don't lose the cache, and (2) removes the `ensureVectorIndex` call from the read path (`performSemanticSearch`) — it's only needed on the write path (`processOutputResult`) since the index must already exist if there are messages to recall. Addresses a user-reported ~15s latency on Upstash Vector.
maintainerNotes: Well-scoped community fix for a real user-reported latency issue (#13952). The logic is sound: read path doesn't need to create the index. CodeRabbit raised a valid concern about the global cache key only using `indexName` (not vector backend identity), which could cause cross-backend conflicts if multiple MastraVector instances share index names — worth evaluating whether this is a realistic scenario. No tests added, but the change is straightforward and low-risk. CI checks are still pending (tests haven't run yet, likely needs maintainer authorization). Vercel deploy also needs authorization. Changeset is included but uses internal phrasing rather than user-facing language per CodeRabbit's suggestion.
